reviews.review_description:
Like the game, but why still christmas theme.
And imaybe some different music. This gets old in 30 seconds and I mute the sound.

Will there be a way to play it is passthrough?
Or is this game no longer maintaint by the devs.

I like the game but it can be a lot better.  I have some suggestions.
- More and bigger puzzle packs
- I would love to have a (laser)pointer to take the pieces.
- Why are all the pieces placed in the right direction? Even on hard. If you take a real puzzle out of the box, most pieces are upside down or in the wrong direction. Shuffle option?
- Some achievements (whatever) to keep you going.

Putting my hand up the whole time is hurting my neck so the laserpoint grap function and shuffle option are two options options that need to be considered.  With a please at the end. 
Thank you.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! Those improvements will definitely come in future updates.

reviews.review_description: This is a rare 6 star game for me.  It could be a 10 star game if one major option were possible...the ability to turn personal photographs into puzzles and an option to select how many  pieces .  That would make this game an absolute must have.

developer_response.body: Really appreciate the 6 stars! Turning personal photos into puzzles is a great idea, it's something we've been exploring, along with piece count customization. No promises yet, but it's definitely on our radar.

reviews.review_title: A different sort of puzzle experience
reviews.review_description:
To see a demo with menu options and how the puzzling works as well as my detailed thoughts, please see my video below. 
You Tube .com /watch?v=SHPS099Ob_A&t=900s
You have to reassemble that link as the system won't let you paste a direct link. 

In brief: for the $1.99 sale price, this is OK,  but it is a long way off from a great puzzle experience. 

In General: 
It gives you random puzzles that seem to be meant to be done rapid fire before moving onto another random puzzle in a very tik tok style. The piece count is mostly miniscule,  I've been given as low as 25 pieces, with the more occasional larger puzzle not to exceed 200 pieces. The art seems very random and AI Generated, some better than others. 

Cons: little choice,  mostly small piece count, generic art, if you close a puzzle and continue it later the pieces will all be moved around, Inability to place pieces in 3D space,  no pointer,  no piece groups (such as in puzzling places), the game gives you repeat puzzles constantly, inability to move the play surface up (the lower lines on the larger puzzle are often so low they are in my legs), highly obnoxious announcer that you cannot turn off separately from the sound effects, only one music track, only one environment.

Pros: a seemingly endless supply of puzzles as long as you keep playing, a very cozy environment to puzzle in, the basic mechanics work very well (only one glitch so far where a piece got locked into place about a foot away from the puzzle, but when the rest of the pieces were in the game still registered it as complete), the one environment is well designed and decorated for the holidays, the one music track is peppy and fun for a bit. 

Hopefully, the developer continues to develop this title with some more options. If it were me my prioritization would be: 1. make a separate option to turn the  announcer off, 2. Stop the game from giving repeat puzzles to people, 3. Supply an actual gallery of puzzles so players have a choice. 4. Make piece count an option, 5. Make larger piece count puzzles, 6. Add a pointer/distance pull option, 7. Memory to piece location in the instance of continuing a puzzle,  8. Make a system for grouping of pieces, 9. Allow us of 3D space for piece organization,  10. Allow the movement of the puzzle board for better placement.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your detailed feedback! We’ll consider your suggestions for future updates. Just a quick note—you can access all puzzles by scrolling through the menu. 😊 Happy puzzling!

reviews.review_description: I love this app but I would love to see more puzzles. A developer responded to another review stating there were about 70 puzzles. I don’t have anything close to that

developer_response.body: Yes, there are around 70 puzzles in total. You can scroll through them by holding the trigger and dragging in the puzzle menu — about 40 are available right away and the other 30 can be unlocked in the store. For the record, if you're reading this and can't see all the puzzles: they are there! When you see the puzzle bubbles, you can drag — it's a scrollable list.

reviews.review_title: Love puzzle game just wish I could
reviews.review_description:
I wish I could purchase games as I want to give me 4 puzzles a month not enough for me I spend hours playing this relaxing game I would like to buy at will 
Thanks

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback and for enjoying Oasis Puzzles! We noticed a bug that stopped players from scrolling through all the puzzles. That’s been fixed, and there are about 70 puzzles to enjoy—no monthly limits! 😊

reviews.review_description: I enjoy making puzzles. It would be a lot easier to have the outline of the puzzle. To where are you put the pieces to stay in the picture and there’s no puzzles that interest me hope to see an update.👍

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We’ll consider adding an outline feature to help with placing pieces more easily. We’re also working on adding more puzzle options in future updates. Stay tuned!
